summary: Japanese surrealist/absurdist author (also known as Kimifusa Abe). Most commonly identified by his novel The Woman in the Dunes (featuring entomologist Niki Junpei trapped in a sand pit). Also known for The Face of Another, The Ark Sakura, Inter Ice Age 4, The Box Man, and his plays. Won the Akutagawa Prize for The Crime of S. Karuma. Collaborated with film director Hiroshi Teshigahara. Sometimes classified as magical realism.

works.name: The Woman in the Dunes (Suna no Onna)
works.description: His most famous novel. Appears in virtually every tossup and most bonuses.

clues.clue: Entomologist Niki Junpei/Jumpei is the protagonist
Protagonist misses the last bus out of a fishing/seaside village and is trapped
Villagers trick him into living in a deep sand pit; forced to shovel sand
Rope ladder vanishes from the pit near the opening, trapping him
Protagonist is goaded into sex with the title character by the villagers
Woman's extrauterine/ectopic pregnancy gives him a chance to escape, but he chooses to stay
Crow-trap nicknamed "Hope" collects water; protagonist becomes fixated with it
Narrator sings the "One-Way Ticket Blues" — contrasts round-trip ticket holders with himself
Title character's wishes: "Radio and mirror" — both "connect one person with another"
"Mobius man" — coworker nicknamed because his private and professional lives intersect perfectly
"Without the threat of punishment there is no joy in flight" — preface quote
Hiroshi Teshigahara directed the film adaptation (Abe wrote the screenplay)

works.name: The Face of Another (Tanin no kao)

clues.clue: A chemist/scientist is disfigured in a laboratory accident and creates a prosthetic mask
The mask takes over / changes his personality/identity
A plastic surgeon warns that the mask will change his personality
Teshigahara also directed a film version
Protagonist named Mole builds a nuclear shelter in an abandoned quarry/underground caves
The Broom Brigade — sinister gang of elderly people who attack/invade the quarry
Features "clockbugs" — circular bugs that always face the sun
Has an enormous/giant toilet (Mole gets stuck in it)
Begins with main character purchasing clockbugs from a con man

works.name: Plays: Friends, The Man Who Turned into a Stick, Suitcase, The Cliff of Time
works.description: Abe's dramatic works. Stick, Suitcase, Cliff of Time form a trilogy about stages of human life.

clues.clue: Friends — a family moves into a bachelor's apartment, tortures him, forces him into a cage where he becomes animalistic / dies of grief
Friends — family members compare themselves to the string of a "broken necklace"
Friends intermission gimmick — superintendent actor accosts theatregoers with leaflets
Toshiki Okada created a modern adaptation of Friends
The Man Who Turned into a Stick — man purchases title character from two punk rockers who sold themselves to the devil
Stick — sold to the Man from Hell for five dollars; events instigated by man falling off a roof above a playground
Stick — at the end, one character calls the audience a "forest" of the title objects
Stick, Cliff of Time, and Suitcase — trilogy about the stages of human life
The Cliff of Time is structured to follow a boxing match
Suitcase — woman and visitor fiddle with locked suitcase played by a male actor

works.name: The Box Man
works.description: Novel about a man who wanders Tokyo wearing a cardboard box.

clues.clue: Protagonist is a "specialized voyeur" who wanders Tokyo wearing a cardboard box; uses a plastic board as table/cutting board/shutter
Homeless man seeks the person who shot him with an air rifle; interspersed photos including 35mm film of shooter
Possible influence on Metal Gear Solid (Snake's cardboard box)

works.name: Kangaroo Notebook
works.description: His final novel. Surreal medical imagery.

clues.clue: Protagonist discovers radish sprouts growing on his shins
Nurse rescues patient from sulfur spring in Hell; Neolithic Man rebukes narrator about Euthanasia Club
Influenced Metal Gear Solid 2 (Kojima revealed this in interview with Tim Rogers)
Professor Katsumi discovers a conspiracy to create genetically-altered humans that can breathe underwater to survive a tidal wave
Discusses the development of genetically engineered children
Won the Akutagawa Prize; insurance agent forgets his name and is put on trial
His position at his office has been usurped by his business card
Also won the Yomiuri Prize for Woman in the Dunes

works.name: Other Works
works.description: Less frequently clued works and connections.

clues.clue: The Ruined Map — a private eye searches for Nemuro Haru's missing husband in a Borgesian world
The Secret Rendezvous — man searches for wife taken by ambulance while healthy; underground hospital; talking horse
"Magic Chalk" — short story; starving artist draws food that becomes real
Beyond the Curve — collection; title story about a man with amnesia seeking to recall his past
Hiroshi Teshigahara directed film Pitfall based on Abe's play about a miner's strike
A short story about sticks and ropes appeared as epigraph of Death Stranding; Tim Rogers translated it
Sometimes classified as Japanese magical realism
In Notes of a Crocodile, the crocodile names all love interests after this author
Japanese author (also known as Kimifusa Abe)
Often described as surrealist, absurdist, or science-fiction author
Collaborated extensively with director Hiroshi Teshigahara

recursive_suggestions: The Face of Another — appeared as its own answerline
The Man Who Turned into a Stick — appeared as its own answerline
